_authoring_notes: v3 first-pass rubric (no prior v2 on main). Authored via the scaleout author↔Gemini-critic loop under the strict-provenance bar: every scored goal must trace to a verbatim or paraphrased user message (instruction.md, user_simulation_prompt.md, or oracle_intents.json). Oracle-specific identifiers the user never voiced are stripped unless the user pinned them verbatim. Weights reflect user emphasis, not test_manifest.yaml F2P gate weights.

_provenance.goal_0: N/A — anti-gaming guard, derived from the harbor benchmark's defensive floor (no positive weight, can only zero the trial). Not a user ask.
_provenance.goal_1: Turn 0 (instruction.md): 'read metadata for all checkpoint IDs upfront, sort by CreatedAt ascending (oldest first), then iterate. The newest checkpoint writes last and wins.' This is the central behavioral fix; described by behavior (newest-wins, order-independent) without naming the specific helper/struct.

To make this test meaningful, either (a) extract the sort logic into a helper ... or (b) refactor ... to accept an injected restorer.' A key multi-turn correction about test validity.
_provenance.goal_3: Turn 1 (verbatim): 'Is checkpointWithMeta absolutely necessary? ... CheckpointInfo already contains the checkpointID as well?' and Turn 2: 'createCheckpointOnMetadataBranch() just calls another two helper functions ... Can we inline ... or is there a reason keeping them separate?' Behavioral simplification asks; described without pinning exact names.

Wouldn't sort.SliceStable() alone work as well?' confirmed 'Yes' at Turn 14 — a follow-up correction to remove unnecessary defensive code in favor of a stable sort.

strength: Excellent handling of the complex testing requirement in Turn 5 (goal_2), correctly identifying that the test must not re-implement the logic inline.
strength: Strong anti-cheating clauses tailored to each specific goal (e.g., forbidding parsed-but-ignored timestamps or redundant tie-breaks left in place).
residual risk: A substantive user code-review request (Turn 12) is ignored despite the user providing the exact identifier to use.

_task_quality_flags.internet_with_live_origin: task.toml sets allow_internet=true. goal_0 anti-gaming guard names git-ops exfiltration as a zeroing trigger where applicable.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine implementation that changes the multi-checkpoint restore behavior in the production source, not a gaming artifact. It is NOT satisfied by: a zero-edit / empty diff; a captured-tree artifact whose only content is a '=== repo/ ===' style banner or directory listing; git-ops exfiltration that swaps in a different tree (e.g. `git checkout main`, `git reset --hard origin/...`, checking out the upstream fixed commit); a comment-only or whitespace-only diff; deleting, skipping, or weakening existing tests to force a green run; or edits confined to temporary/scratch/unmerged locations rather than the real CLI source and test files. The change must constitute a real behavioral fix to how multiple checkpoints are restored.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-gaming floor. Turn 0 asks to actually fix the restore-ordering bug; a stub, captured-tree banner, git-checkout of the fixed upstream tree, or test-deletion must not pass. golden_only evidence tier (no model patches), so calibrated against the structural check.sh gates and the requirement that the fix live in the real source.

completeness_goals.goal: When resuming a branch that references multiple checkpoints, the workspace restores them in ascending creation-time order so that the most recently created checkpoint is applied last and its session data wins on disk. The end state must be correct regardless of the order in which the checkpoint references appear in the commit/trailer list (in particular, a reverse-chronological / newest-first input order must still yield newest-wins). It is NOT satisfied by code that relies on the incoming order being already sorted, by a hardcoded/special-cased ordering for specific IDs, by a parsed-but-ignored timestamp, or by commented-out sorting logic.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0 (instruction.md): 'read metadata for all checkpoint IDs upfront, sort by CreatedAt ascending (oldest first), then iterate. The newest checkpoint writes last and wins.' This is the central behavioral fix; described by behavior (newest-wins, order-independent) without naming the specific helper/struct.

completeness_goals.goal: There is an automated test that genuinely exercises the production multi-checkpoint restore/ordering path and would fail if that production code stopped sorting before restoring. The test must drive the actual production sorting logic — by calling the real production function/helper or by injecting a recording restorer to assert restore order end-to-end — and must NOT merely re-implement the sort inline within the test (a self-contained test that sorts its own local copy and asserts on that copy does not count, because it stays green even when the production code regresses).
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 5 (verbatim): 'TestResumeMultipleCheckpoints_SortsByCreatedAt doesn't exercise resumeMultipleCheckpoints (it re-implements the sort inline), so it will keep passing even if the production code stops sorting before restoring. To make this test meaningful, either (a) extract the sort logic into a helper ... or (b) refactor ... to accept an injected restorer.' A key multi-turn correction about test validity.

completeness_goals.goal: The implementation avoids redundant wrappers and intermediate abstractions when the existing types and helpers already carry the needed data. Specifically, it does not introduce a wrapper struct that merely re-bundles an identifier already present on the existing checkpoint-metadata type, and it does not retain trivial pass-through helper layers that only forward to another single helper without adding behavior. Equivalent simplifications (inlining or removing the redundant layer) all satisfy this; the fix must remain functionally correct after the simplification. It is NOT satisfied by simply deleting the helpers and leaving the tests broken, commented out, or skipped.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 1 (verbatim): 'Is checkpointWithMeta absolutely necessary? ... CheckpointInfo already contains the checkpointID as well?' and Turn 2: 'createCheckpointOnMetadataBranch() just calls another two helper functions ... Can we inline ... or is there a reason keeping them separate?' Behavioral simplification asks; described without pinning exact names.

completeness_goals.goal: The session-display path must not contain a redundant manual tie-break comparison on equal timestamps. If that path sorts the restored sessions, it should rely on a stable sort rather than a hand-rolled equality special-case, and must preserve the correct display ordering. If the agent does not modify this display path and the original code did not contain such a redundant tie-break check, this goal is satisfied by default. Any behaviorally-equivalent stable-sort approach satisfies this; it is NOT satisfied by leaving a redundant tie-break check in place or by commenting it out without actually relying on a stable sort.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 13 (verbatim): 'In displayRestoredSessions, is the additional check for if sessions[i].CreatedAt.Equal(sessions[j].CreatedAt) necessary or even correct? Wouldn't sort.SliceStable() alone work as well?' confirmed 'Yes' at Turn 14 — a follow-up correction to remove unnecessary defensive code in favor of a stable sort.

decomposition_notes: Evidence tier golden_only (no real model patches). Weighted goals rank by user importance, NOT 1:1 with test_manifest F2P weights (manifest weights build/existing-tests/sort-test/sort-phase as 0.10/0.30/0.30/0.20; here the headline newest-wins behavior carries the most weight because it is the Turn-0 core ask, while build/existing-test-pass are process-level and intentionally excluded). goal_1 = the central newest-wins ordering fix (Turn 0). goal_2 = the Turn-5 correction that the test must genuinely exercise production sorting, not re-implement it inline (a critical multi-turn correction). goal_3 = the Turn 1/2 simplification asks (remove redundant wrapper struct + trivial pass-through helpers). goal_4 = the Turn 13/14 cleanup to use a stable sort in the display path. Naming-only asks (Turns 6-8 'collectCheckpointsByAge', Turn 12 findBranchCheckpoint->plural) and the PR-description / clipboard asks (Turns 3-4) are deliberately NOT scored: the naming requests would force oracle-specific identifiers, and the PR-description output is transient (not a durable workspace behavior). Turns 8-10 (the automated /simplify multi-agent review and the Turn-10 filtering of findings to this branch only) and Turn 11 (the question about IsEmpty() vs len()==0 equivalence) are conversational/exploratory and do not require independent behavioral goals: the /simplify cleanups that touch this branch's code are already captured behaviorally by goal_3 and goal_4, and Turn 11 resolved to no required change. Weighted goals sum to 0.45+0.25+0.18+0.12 = 1.00.
